autoScan.label: Enable automatic scans
autoScan.help: When enabled, all configured paths will be scanned automatically at the specified interval

scanInterval.label: Scan interval (hours)
scanInterval.help: How often the automatic scan should run (minimum 1 hour)

daysUntilStale.label: Days until 'stale' (warning)
daysUntilStale.help: Datapoints not updated for this many days are marked as 'stale'

daysUntilDead.label: Days until 'dead' (critical)
daysUntilDead.help: Datapoints not updated for this many days are marked as 'dead'

_pathsInfo.text: Configure which paths to scan for unused datapoints. Each path will get its own result state with a JSON table.

items.title: Check alias targets
items.tooltip: For alias.* paths: Check if target datapoints exist (ghost detection)

_fieldInfo.text:
Each scan result contains a JSON array with the following fields per datapoint:

• id - Full datapoint path (unique identification)
• name - common.name or last part of ID (user-friendly name)
• last_ts - Unix timestamp in ms or null (for sorting)
• last_ts_iso - Formatted date string (for display)
• value - Current datapoint value (final check before deletion)
• status - active, dead, stale, undefined, orphaned (classification in English)
• status_de - aktiv, inaktiv, veraltet, undefiniert, verwaist (classification in German)
• issue - dead, stale, orphaned_alias, or null (filter criterion in English)
• issue_de - inaktiv, veraltet, verwaistes Alias, or null (filter criterion in German)
• size - JSON.stringify(val).length (finds storage hogs)

_usageInfo.text: For German tables, use the '_de' fields (status_de, issue_de) in your VIS widget column configuration. For English tables, use the regular fields (status, issue). Both field sets are always included in the JSON output.
